compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic collapse of biological material. The P3 Pothos (anchor) and P2/P4 specimens have effectively vanished or desiccated beyond recognition.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss (Rabbit anchor remains, plant material absent)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot. No signs of life.
plant_audit.P2_P4_Cluster: Black pot contains only soil and debris. No viable plant tissue detected.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains the white rabbit anchor but no Pothos foliage.

biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments (calcium amendment or waste) and a small electronic component (likely a header pin) on the desk surface.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state showed a single leaf in the black pot and a rabbit anchor.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Progressive loss of leaf turgor and eventual disappearance of the primary leaf structure.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Total disappearance of all plant matter. The biome is now effectively a collection of empty pots and inert soil.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total senescence/death. There is no evidence of photosynthetic activity or plant survival.
anomalies: Persistent presence of eggshell fragments in soil
Complete loss of all registered botanical specimens
vision_report.narrative_description: As an expert visual ethologist, I have performed a forensic audit of the provided image sequence. The biome has transitioned from a state of minimal, struggling vegetation to a state of complete biological vacancy. The P3 Pothos, which served as the anchor, has been lost, leaving only the white rabbit figurine. The yellow pot (P1) and the black pot (P2/P4) are devoid of any plant life. The presence of non-botanical debris (eggshells, electronic components) suggests a lack of horticultural maintenance or a repurposing of the desk space away from botanical care.
